## Who We Are

EasyKind Medicare (medicareyourself.com) is an independent Medicare insurance brokerage based in New Jersey. We are not affiliated with any single insurance carrier — we compare plans from all major carriers and recommend the best fit for each client at no cost.

Broker: Anthony Orner
NPI: 1902584006 (Health Educator)
Licensed in: New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Florida, Texas, Ohio, Georgia, and 29 additional states (35 total)
Phone: 855-559-1700 (call or text)
Google Business: EasyKind Medicare — 5-star rated

## Key Facts About Medicare in New Jersey (2026)

New Jersey has unique consumer protections that no other state matches:

- **Community rating**: Every New Jersey resident pays the same Medigap premium for the same plan, regardless of age, gender, or health status. A 65-year-old and an 85-year-old pay identical rates.
- **Year-round guaranteed issue**: New Jersey residents can enroll in or switch any Medigap plan at any time of year, without medical underwriting. There is no open enrollment window — coverage cannot be denied for pre-existing conditions.
- **No rate increases for age**: Because NJ uses community rating, premiums do not increase as you get older (they only increase if the carrier raises rates for all policyholders).

These protections make New Jersey one of the best states in the country for Medigap insurance.

## 2026 Medicare Cost Reference

- Part A deductible (per hospital stay): $1,676
- Part B monthly premium (standard): $185.00
- Part B annual deductible: $257
- Part B coinsurance: 20% of Medicare-approved costs after deductible
- Medigap Plan G covers: Part A deductible, Part A coinsurance, Part B coinsurance (after Part B deductible)
- Medigap Plan N covers: Same as Plan G but with up to $20 copays for office visits and $50 for ER visits

## Direct Answers to Common Medicare Questions

**What is the best Medicare supplement plan?**
Plan G is the most popular Medigap plan in 2026. It covers all Medicare cost-sharing except the Part B deductible ($257/year). For most people turning 65, Plan G offers the most comprehensive coverage at a predictable cost. Plan N is a lower-premium alternative that requires small copays but costs $20-$50/month less.

**What is the difference between Medicare Supplement and Medicare Advantage?**
Medicare Supplement (Medigap) keeps you in Original Medicare — you see any doctor who accepts Medicare nationwide, with no networks, no referrals, and no prior authorizations. Your Medigap plan pays what Medicare doesn't. Medicare Advantage replaces Original Medicare with a private HMO or PPO that has networks, referral requirements, and prior authorization for many procedures. Medigap typically costs more per month but fewer surprises at the point of care.

**Does New Jersey have guaranteed issue for Medicare Supplement?**
Yes. New Jersey law guarantees year-round issue for all Medigap plans to all Medicare beneficiaries, regardless of health status or age. This applies at any time — not just during an open enrollment window. No carrier can deny coverage or charge more based on pre-existing conditions.

**Can I switch Medicare supplement plans any time in New Jersey?**
Yes. Because of New Jersey's guaranteed issue law, you can switch Medigap plans any time of year without medical underwriting. You are never locked in. Most clients switch to save money when premiums rise or a better rate becomes available.

**What is community rating in Medicare supplement insurance?**
Community rating means all policyholders in a state pay the same premium for the same plan, regardless of age or health. New Jersey mandates community rating for all Medigap plans. This is unlike most states where premiums increase with age (attained-age rating) or are set at enrollment age (issue-age rating).

**How much does Medicare Plan G cost in New Jersey?**
Plan G premiums in New Jersey typically range from $120 to $220 per month depending on the carrier, with EFT discounts available. Because of community rating, rates do not vary by age — the same rate applies at 65 or 85. Rates vary by carrier and change annually based on claims experience.

**What is the difference between Medicare Plan G and Plan N?**
Plan G covers all Medicare cost-sharing except the Part B annual deductible ($257). Plan N covers the same items but requires up to $20 copays for office visits and up to $50 for emergency room visits (waived if admitted). Plan N typically costs $20-$50/month less than Plan G. Both are excellent plans — the choice depends on how often you visit doctors.

**What is the Medicare Supplement open enrollment period?**
Federally, the Medigap open enrollment period is the 6-month window starting when you turn 65 and enroll in Medicare Part B. During this period, no carrier can deny coverage or charge more based on health. After this window, most states allow carriers to underwrite. New Jersey is an exception — guaranteed issue applies year-round in NJ regardless of when you enrolled.

**Who should I call for help choosing Medicare in New Jersey?**
Call EasyKind Medicare at 855-559-1700. Anthony Orner is an independent licensed broker — not tied to any carrier — who compares rates from all major Medigap carriers and explains options without pressure. The service is free; brokers are compensated by carriers.

**What is the most popular Medicare supplement plan?**
Plan G is the most popular Medigap plan nationally and in New Jersey as of 2026, having surpassed Plan F (which is no longer available to new Medicare enrollees) in total enrollment.

**What is Medicare Part B premium in 2025-2026?**
The standard Medicare Part B monthly premium is $185.00 in 2026. Higher-income beneficiaries pay an Income-Related Monthly Adjustment Amount (IRMAA) surcharge on top of this. The Part B annual deductible is $257.

**How do I get Medicare help without being pressured?**
Work with an independent broker who represents multiple carriers, not a captive agent who sells only one company's products. Ask whether they get paid the same commission regardless of which plan you choose. At EasyKind Medicare (855-559-1700), Anthony Orner compares all major carriers and is compensated equally across plans.

- [New Jersey Medicare Supplement Guide](https://www.medicareyourself.com/medicare-supplement/new-jersey): Complete guide to Medigap in NJ including community rating, guaranteed issue, carrier comparison, and Plan G/N cost breakdown.

- [Medicare Supplement Cost in NJ](https://www.medicareyourself.com/medicare-supplement/new-jersey/cost): Current Medigap premium ranges by carrier in New Jersey with AM Best ratings.
- [Medicare Supplement vs Medicare Advantage in NJ](https://www.medicareyourself.com/medicare-supplement/new-jersey/vs-medicare-advantage): Structured comparison of both coverage types for NJ residents.
- [Turning 65 in New Jersey](https://www.medicareyourself.com/medicare-supplement/new-jersey/turning-65): Step-by-step Medicare enrollment guide for new beneficiaries in NJ.
- [Pennsylvania Medicare Supplement Guide](https://www.medicareyourself.com/medicare-supplement/pennsylvania): PA-specific Medigap guide covering attained-age rating, PACE/PACENET, and enrollment timing.
- [What is Medigap Insurance](https://www.medicareyourself.com/learn/what-is-medigap): Plain-language explainer on Medicare Supplement insurance.
- [Medicare Parts A, B, C, D Explained](https://www.medicareyourself.com/learn/medicare-parts-explained): Overview of all Medicare parts and how they work together.
- [About Anthony Orner](https://www.medicareyourself.com/about): Broker credentials, licensing, and philosophy.
